paper.title_en: Conformally Invariant Corrections to the Anomaly-Induced Effective Action and Black Hole Evaporation in Four Dimensions
paper.abstract_en: When matter fields are integrated out in a large N approximation, the conformal anomaly induces an effective action up to conformally invariant correction terms. In the present work, we consider the implications on black hole evaporation of such a term involving a conformal invariant found by Fefferman and Graham. Working in an approximation where the spacetime is static, we compute the induced stress tensor around a Schwarzschild black hole. The boundary conditions select an Unruh-like asymptotic sector, but not a unique stress tensor. A new one-parameter family of quantum hair emerges which changes the stress tensor in the near-horizon region. This suggests a new semiclassical mechanism by which information about black hole formation could be encoded outside the horizon.
